---
title: "Research records"
---

## Academic work

:::{#tab-fund}

<a id="tb1"></a>*Table 2. Scholarships and Research Funding*

| Paper | Status | Journal ranking |
|---------------|------|---------------|
| **Cao, T. T.**, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S. (2025). Financial self-discrepancy, consumer well-being, and luxury preferences: The moderating effect of access-based luxury formats. *Psychology & Marketing*. [https://doi.org/10.1002/mar.70057](https://doi.org/10.1002/mar.70057) | Published | Q1 JCR, A-ranked ABDC |
| **Cao, T. T.**, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S. (2026). Conflicted by faith, drawn to pleasure: How religious self-discrepancy shapes product choices. *International Journal of Consumer Studies* [https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70202](https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70202) |  Published | Q1 JCR, A-ranked ABDC |

:::

## Published and In Press

- **Cao, T. T.**, Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S. (2025). Compensatory consumption: A review and research agenda using the Theory‐Context‐Characteristics‐Methodology framework. *International Journal of Consumer Studies*, *49*(5), e70112.[https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70112](https://doi.org/10.1111/ijcs.70112)


- Cao, T. T. (2025). Self-discrepancy and gambling retention: A conceptual model of motivational and moral pathways. *Strategic Business Research*, 100001.[https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sbr.2025.100001](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.sbr.2025.100001)

- Cao, T. (2025). Navigating dual identities: How bicultural consumers leverage luxury consumption to cope with perceived discrimination and enhance well-being. *Journal of Development and Integration* (In Press).

- Ha, N. M., & Tuan, C. N. (2019). Constructing athlete identification and the effectiveness of athlete endorsement on customer's purchase intention. *Journal of Distribution Science*, *17*(8), 87–97.[http://dx.doi.org/10.15722/jds.17.8.201908.87](http://dx.doi.org/10.15722/jds.17.8.201908.87)

## Under Review and Revision

- Cao, T., Prentice, C., Wang, Q., & Nguyen, H. S. (2025). Conflicted by faith, drawn to pleasure: How religious self-discrepancy shapes product choices. *International Journal of Consumer Studies* (second round of revision).

- Cao, T. (2025). From waste to worth: How true-to-ideal authenticity, actual-ought discrepancy, and user-generated content shape willingness to pay for upcycled food. *Journal of Consumer Behaviour* (First submission).
